channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government invests in bridge to improve safety and transport along Mlima Nyoka bypass
item.description: The Tanzanian government has earmarked over Sh482 million for constructing a modern bridge in Uyole Valley, Mbeya, to improve safety and transport connectivity along the accident-prone Mlima Nyoka byp...

item.title: Petition filed to disqualify three BARMM MPs over alleged dual party membership
item.description: A formal petition was filed before the Commission on Elections Bangsamoro Registration and Accreditation Committee seeking the disqualification of three incumbent Members of Parliament of the Bangsamo...

item.title: Clarifying Labor’s Budget Changes: CGT, Negative Gearing and Testamentary Trusts
item.description: Last week’s Australian budget prompted an over-the-top reaction from conservative media and politicians, focusing on unfounded fears about housing market collapse and so-called 'death taxes'. Concerns...

item.title: HuffPost 2026 Shopping Award Winners Now on Memorial Day Sale
item.description: HuffPost has announced the winners of its 2026 Shopping Awards, highlighting 74 top-rated products across tech, home, beauty, pet care, and fashion. Many of these award-winning items are currently ava...

item.title: Saskatchewan Women’s Groups Applaud Supreme Court Ruling
item.description: Several women’s organisations in Regina, Saskatchewan, have praised a recent Supreme Court ruling considered a significant milestone for gender equality and women’s rights in Canada. The decision, whi...

item.title: Kenya High Court rules against criminalising consensual under-age sex, calls for better sex education
item.description: The Kenya High Court has ruled that minors should no longer face criminal prosecution for consensual sexual activity, emphasising the importance of access to proper sex education. Rights campaigners h...

item.title: Jerusalem and Kuse Set for Deciding Third Match in WBC Minimumweight Division
item.description: Filipino boxer Melvin Jerusalem is preparing for a third showdown against South African Siyakholwa Kuse after losing his WBC minimumweight title in their second fight on May 16. Jerusalem, whose recor...

item.title: Children’s Society garden at Chelsea Flower Show celebrates beauty in imperfection for young people’s mental health
item.description: The Children’s Society has won a gold medal at the RHS Chelsea Flower Show for a garden designed to highlight the beauty found in imperfection, particularly relevant for supporting teenagers’ mental w...

item.title: Bwala Explains Nigerians’ Right to Self-Defence Amid Rising Insecurity
item.description: Daniel Bwala, the Special Adviser to President Bola Tinubu on Policy Communication, don talk say the Nigerian Constitution dey give every citizen di right to defend demself. For one interview with Ari...

item.title: Kaduna APC Leaders Emerge Unopposed Through Consensus Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: For Kaduna State, di Speaker of di State House of Assembly, Dahiru Liman, plus di Deputy Speaker, Henry Danjuma, and di Majority Leader, Munira Tanimu, don secure APC tickets for their constituencies ...

item.title: Gold Prices Climb Following U.S.-Iran Diplomacy Developments
item.description: Gold prices rose by up to 1.6 per cent after U.S. President Donald Trump indicated that the United States is in the 'final stages' of negotiations with Iran. This statement boosted optimism that the M...

item.title: Analysis on Senator Bato dela Rosa's Fugitive Status and Arrest Challenges
item.description: The episode of In the Public Square discusses the situation of Senator Bato dela Rosa, who has been evading arrest for six months after an ICC arrest warrant was issued against him. The Solicitor Gene...

item.title: Desingerica criticizes Eurovision, calls competition unfair despite liking the winning song
item.description: Serbian rapper and artist Desingerica openly criticized this year's Eurovision Song Contest, calling it unfair and rigged. Speaking during the filming of the music competition show 'Pinkove zvezde,' D...

item.title: Many GOP voters in Kentucky rely solely on Trump's endorsement, unaware of candidates’ records
item.description: In the recent election in Kentucky, a significant number of Republican voters who supported Ed Gallrein over incumbent Rep. Thomas Massie demonstrated limited knowledge about the candidates' political...

item.title: NUT Raises Alarm Over Rising School Attacks, Calls For Stronger Security
item.description: The Nigeria Union of Teachers (NUT) don speak strong against recent wahala wey dey affect schools, teachers and students for parts of Nigeria. Dem describe the attacks as barbaric and inhuman, especia...

item.title: Authorities Review Manifesto Linked to San Diego Mosque Attack Suspects Containing Hate Speech
item.description: Law enforcement officials are examining a 75-page document allegedly authored by two teenagers, Caleb Vasquez, 18, and Cain Clark, 17, who are suspected of killing three people outside the Islamic Cen...

item.title: Comparison of Japanese Novel The Devotion of Suspect X and Malayalam Film Drishyam
item.description: This article compares the popular Japanese crime novel 'The Devotion of Suspect X' by Keigo Higashino with the 2013 Malayalam film 'Drishyam' directed by Jeethu Joseph. The novel, published in 2005, t...

item.title: Mother Appeals for Justice After Losing Three Children in Fatal Accident
item.description: A Kenyan mother is mourning the loss of her three children following a tragic road accident that occurred on Friday, May 15, in Rivers State. Her three children, aged three, four, and ten, were killed...

item.title: Explaining Labor’s Changes to Capital Gains Tax, Negative Gearing and Trusts
item.description: Labor’s recent adjustments to investor tax breaks have sparked an intense reaction in political and media circles. The reforms primarily target capital gains tax discounts, negative gearing, and the u...

item.title: The Iconic Heart-and-Dagger Sweaters of 1940s Hollywood
item.description: In the 1940s, the 'heart-and-dagger' or 'bleeding heart' sweater became a notable trend in novelty knitwear, largely popularized by Los Angeles designer Suse (Susan) Dannenberg. Seeking to draw attent...
